windows.transcript: And while you are melting your wax you're going to want something to stir it with and also a digital thermometer to keep track of the temperature Once your
windows.embed_text_v2: Presenting candle-making tools, hand holding a digital thermometer and metal stir stick over an overhead flat-lay of white soy wax flakes in a metal bowl, amber fragrance oil bottles, black digital scale, stainless pouring pitcher and wooden wicks on a wood table.
windows.summary_v2: Overhead flat-lay where a hand presents a digital thermometer and a metal stirring tool among laid-out candle-making supplies including soy wax flakes, fragrance oils, scale and pouring pitcher.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead flat-lay of candle-making supplies on a wood table: black digital scale, large metal bowl of white soy wax flakes, stainless pouring pitcher, two amber fragrance oil bottles, spoon, wooden wicks and a glass jar.
A hand enters from the bottom holding a digital thermometer, laying its metal probe across the black scale among the laid-out tools.
The hand holds the digital thermometer in one hand and picks up a long metal stir stick with the other, presenting both over the supplies.
Hand displays the digital thermometer beside a metal stirring spatula held vertically over the amber oil bottles, showing the temperature-checking and stirring tools.
Hand continues holding up the digital thermometer and the metal stir tool, gesturing toward the bowl of soy wax flakes and pouring pitcher.
The metal stir stick is held upright near the fragrance bottles while the other hand keeps the digital thermometer in view over the scale.
Hand lowers the stir tool slightly, still presenting the digital thermometer at the bottom of the frame among the candle-making equipment.
Hand sets the stir stick down by the spoon and holds the digital thermometer at the lower edge, full array of wax, oils, pitcher and wicks visible.
